title: 10 best herbal tinctures for grief
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for grief are concentrated plant extracts that have been used for centuries to support emotional well-being and alleviate symptoms of grief. These potent brews work by gently toning the nervous system, calming the mind, and soothing the heart. Examples of herbal tinctures that can help with grief include Ashwagandha, known for its adaptogenic properties that reduce stress and anxiety, and St. John's Wort, which has been shown to lift mood and promote feelings of joy. By incorporating these tinctures into their daily routine, individuals can find solace in the natural world and begin to heal from loss, ultimately improving their overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with grief because it promotes a sense of calmness and relaxation, allowing individuals to process their emotions more effectively. The herb's sedative properties help reduce anxiety and stress, creating an environment for introspection and emotional release. Additionally, valerian tinctures can aid in improving sleep quality, which is often disrupted during times of grief. By promoting a restful night's sleep, individuals can wake up feeling refreshed and more able to cope with their emotions, ultimately facilitating the grieving process.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anxiolytic effects: Valerian tincture helps to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, which can be beneficial in managing the emotional turmoil often associated with grief.

Adaptogenic properties: Valerian tincture has adaptogenic effects, meaning it helps the body adjust to stress and promotes balance, which can help alleviate feelings of overwhelm and emotional exhaustion that often accompany grief.

Analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian tincture contains compounds that have analgesic (pain-relieving) and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce physical and emotional pain associated with grief, such as a heavy heart or tears.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: A sesquiterpene acid found in Valeriana officinalis, valerenic acid has been shown to have sedative and anxiolytic properties, helping to calm the mind and body during times of grief.
Isovaleric acid: This organic acid acts as a GABA receptor agonist, which can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier to cope with emotional pain associated with grief.

Valeranone: A valerenic acid derivative, valeranone has been found to possess sedative and muscle relaxant properties, potentially reducing the physical tension and emotional distress that often accompanies grief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for the plant's sedative and anxiolytic properties.
Roots: Contain valerianic acid, which helps to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.
Roots: High in valepotriates, a class of compounds that contribute to the plant's sedative and muscle relaxant effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is at its peak potency level.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dry place for 1 to 2 weeks to remove excess moisture content.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine them with 80 to 100 proof vodka in a 1 to 5 ratio.
Store the mixture in a glass jar and allow it to steep for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with grief because of its adaptogenic properties, allowing it to address the complex emotional landscape that often accompanies loss. The herb's constituents, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to modulate mood and reduce feelings of sadness and anxiety. By promoting a sense of calm and emotional balance, St John's Wort tinctures can provide comfort during times of mourning, helping individuals process their emotions and begin the healing journey.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: St. John's Wort has been shown to adapt to an individual's specific needs, reducing anxiety and stress while promoting emotional resilience during times of grief.

Antidepressant properties: The herb contains compounds like hyperforin and hypericin, which have been found to have antidepressant effects by increasing neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, helping alleviate symptoms of depression that may accompany grief.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to feelings of emotional numbness or despair during times of grief.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This phenolic compound has been shown to have a positive effect on mood regulation and may help alleviate symptoms of grief by promoting a sense of calm and reducing anxiety.
Hypericin: As a potent antioxidant and phenolic compound, hypericin may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to feelings of grief and emotional pain.

Flavonoids (including rutin and quercetin): These polyphenolic compounds have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with grief, promoting emotional balance and well-being.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antidepressant properties, helping to alleviate grief-related emotional pain.
Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and hyperforin, the leaves have a calming effect on the nervous system, aiding in the management of grief and promoting emotional balance.

Roots: The roots of Hypericum perforatum contain a higher concentration of hypericin and pseudohypericin, which have been shown to have antidepressant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe grief and emotional distress.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part dried hypericum perforatum flowers and 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Combine the ingredients and seal the jar leaving 1 inch of space at the top.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks shaking it daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.
Discard the solids and store the herbal tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with grief because it has a profound impact on the emotional body, allowing individuals to process and release pent-up emotions. The herb's natural adaptogenic properties help to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress that often accompany loss. Additionally, maypop's subtle sedative effects promote relaxation and clarity, enabling individuals to confront their grief in a peaceful and reflective state. This allows for a deeper understanding and acceptance of their emotions, facilitating a more rapid and natural healing process.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Maypop's anxiolytic effects can help alleviate feelings of anxiety and worry associated with grief, promoting relaxation and reducing emotional overwhelm.

Sedative properties: The sedative properties of Maypop can aid in calming the nervous system, reducing stress and insomnia that may be triggered by grief, and promoting a sense of calm and tranquility.

Adaptogenic properties: Maypop's adaptogenic effects help the body respond to stress and emotional trauma caused by grief, allowing it to better cope with the emotional burden and find a sense of balance and resilience.
remedy_constituents: Harmane: Harmane is a beta-carboline alkaloid that has been shown to have anxiolytic and sedative properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of grief-related anxiety and insomnia.
Isoferulic acid: Isoferulic acid is a phenolic acid that has been found to have a sedative effect and may help reduce stress and anxiety related to grief, promoting relaxation and calmness.

Apigenin: Apigenin is a flavonoid that has been shown to have anxiolytic and sedative properties, which can help reduce anxiety and stress related to grief, promoting relaxation and reducing emotional distress.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used for their sedative and anxiolytic properties, which help alleviate symptoms of grief and stress.
Leaves: They contain a high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids, which contribute to their calming effects and are often used in tinctures for grief.
Fruits: The pulp of the fruits is used, as it contains a high amount of passifloric acid, which has been traditionally used to help manage stress and anxiety associated with grief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves on a sunny day in late summer.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 2 days.
Combine the dried plant material with 500 milliliters of 45% ethanol and stir well.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar for 4 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with grief because it has a profound calming effect on the mind and body. The herb's soothing properties can help to ease emotional turmoil, promoting a sense of serenity and tranquility in those struggling with loss. By reducing anxiety and stress levels, English lavender tincture allows individuals to process their emotions more effectively, fostering a deeper connection with themselves and the world around them. This, in turn, can facilitate a healthier grieving process and ultimately lead to emotional healing.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Lavender's sedative properties can help to calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, which can be particularly helpful during times of grief.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with emotional pain, such as grief, allowing for a more balanced emotional response.

Anxiolytic and antidepressant effects: Lavender has been shown to have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and antidepressant effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of depression and anxiety that often accompany grief.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene found in Lavandula angustifolia tinctures, linalool has a calming and sedative effect, which can help alleviate emotional distress and promote relaxation in individuals experiencing grief.

Linalyl acetate: Another terpene present in Lavandula angustifolia tinctures, linalyl acetate has a soothing effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting a sense of calmness, which can be beneficial during periods of grief.

Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound found in Lavandula angustifolia tinctures, rosmarinic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce emotional pain and inflammation associated with grief.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their calming and soothing properties, which can help alleviate emotional distress associated with grief.
Leaves: They contain essential oils with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in emotional healing and reduction of stress related to grief.
Stems: They contribute to the overall medicinal properties of the plant, providing a subtle yet supportive effect for emotional balance during times of grief.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and 750ml of high-proof alcohol like ethanol or glycerin.
Combine the dried flowers with the alcohol or glycerin in a clean glass jar to create a mixture.
Steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar occasionally to aid infusion.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to remove the solids.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milkweed tinctures helps with grief because of its unique ability to support emotional resilience. The plant's natural compounds, such as alkaloids and saponins, work to soothe and calm the nervous system, allowing individuals to process and integrate their emotions in a healthy way. As a result, milkweed tinctures can help reduce feelings of anxiety, depression, and despair that often accompany grief, promoting a sense of peace and acceptance. By supporting emotional equilibrium, milkweed tinctures provide a gentle yet powerful tool for navigating the challenging landscape of grief.
remedy_properties: Alkaloids: Milkweed tincture contains alkaloids, which have been shown to help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, allowing individuals to better cope with emotional pain and grief.

Saponins: The saponin content in milkweed tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe emotional trauma and reduce feelings of overwhelm, making it easier for individuals to process their emotions and grieve.

Triterpenes: Triterpenes present in milkweed tincture have been found to exhibit sedative and anxiolytic effects, which can help calm the nervous system and promote a sense of peace and tranquility, allowing individuals to better navigate the grieving process.
remedy_constituents: Cardenolides (Cytotoxic Glycosides): These compounds, particularly cardenolide C, may help alleviate grief by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to emotional distress.

Naphthoquinones (Anthraquinones): Naphthoquinones, such as asclepiodine, may aid in grief recovery by acting as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agents, helping to calm the nervous system and promote emotional balance.

Flavonoids (Quercetin, Kaempferol): The flavonoids present in Asclepias syriaca, like quercetin and kaempferol, may help alleviate grief by exhibiting anti-anxiety and anti-depressant properties, reducing stress and promoting a sense of calmness.
remedy_parts: Roots: As a source of compounds that may help with emotional healing and stress relief.
Leaves: As a source of compounds that may help with anxiety and stress relief.
Flowers: As a source of compounds that may help with emotional healing and mood elevation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh or 3 parts of dried asclepias syriaca roots and stems in the early morning or late afternoon.
Chop the asclepias syriaca roots and stems into small pieces weighing about 1 kg or 2.2 pounds.
Combine the chopped asclepias syriaca with 3 parts of 95% grain alcohol in a clean 1-liter glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking it every week to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with grief because its subtle yet potent essence gently calms the emotional turmoil that often accompanies loss. The ancient plant's properties have been traditionally used to soothe the heart and mind, promoting a sense of balance and clarity in the face of sorrow. As the tincture works to ease emotional distress, it allows individuals to process their feelings with greater ease, ultimately leading to a more peaceful and accepting acceptance of their loss.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ginkgo biloba tincture helps the body adapt to stress and emotional turmoil, promoting resilience in the face of adversity and reducing feelings of overwhelm.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant compounds in Maidenhair Tree tincture may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation associated with grief-related anxiety and depression, promoting a sense of calm and well-being.

Vasodilatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's ability to dilate blood vessels may enhance circulation and oxygenation to the brain, which can improve cognitive function, memory, and emotional processing, potentially aiding in the processing of grief and emotions.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This triterpene helps alleviate stress and anxiety, which can often accompany grief, by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.

Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin, kaempferol): These phenolic compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help mitigate the emotional and psychological distress associated with grief by reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being.

Ginkgolides (e.g., ginkgolide A, B): These sesquiterpene lactones have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of depression and anxiety that can accompany grief, by protecting neurons and reducing inflammation in the brain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are used due to their ability to support mood balance and emotional healing.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used for their potential to promote emotional release and grief resolution.
Buds: Ginkgo biloba buds are used to support heart healing and emotional renewal, which can be helpful during times of grief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in the fall when they are mature and dry the leaves thoroughly.
Dry the leaves further in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Measure out 100 grams of the dried ginkgo biloba leaves and add them to a jar.
Add 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka to the jar and seal it tightly for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats tinctures helps with grief because they soothe the emotional turmoil that often accompanies loss. The calming properties of oats tinctures can ease feelings of anxiety, fear, and overwhelm, allowing individuals to process their emotions more smoothly. Antioxidant properties: Oats are rich in antioxidants, including phenolic acids and flavonoids, which help to protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by grief, allowing for a smoother emotional processing of emotions and reduced feelings of despair.

Soothing and emollient effects on the nervous system: Oat tinctures contain soothing compounds that can calm and quiet the nervous system, providing relief from anxiety, stress, and overwhelm often experienced during periods of grief, allowing for a more peaceful and restorative sleep.
remedy_constituents: Avenacoside: Avenacoside has been shown to have anxiolytic and sedative properties, helping to reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can be beneficial in managing grief.
Avenalumic acid: Avenalumic acid has been found to have a sedative effect, which can help reduce anxiety and promote a sense of calm, making it easier to cope with the emotional pain of grief.

Ferulic acid: Ferulic acid has antioxidant properties and may help reduce inflammation, which can contribute to the emotional and physical symptoms associated with grief, such as anxiety, depression, and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used for their calming and sedative properties to help ease emotional distress associated with grief.
Leaves: They are used for their nervine and adaptogenic properties to help the body and mind adapt to stress and emotional turmoil.
Seeds: They are used for their anxiolytic properties to help alleviate anxiety and promote emotional balance during times of grief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of the dried roots of avena sativa on a sunny day after 2 years of maturity.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces and store them in an airtight container for 2 weeks.
Combine the chopped roots with 40-50% vodka in a 1:5 ratio and store in a glass jar for 2 months.
Shake the jar daily for 2 minutes to ensure the roots are fully saturated with the vodka for 60 days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the avena sativa tincture.

remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate emotional stress and promote a sense of calm, which can be beneficial during periods of grief.

Phenolic acids (e.g., chlorogenic acid and ellagic acid): These compounds have been shown to have a neuroprotective effect, which may help mitigate the emotional impact of grief by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.

Buds: Contain bioflavonoids and other compounds that may help stabilize mood and reduce feelings of sadness and despair associated with grief.
remedy_recipe: Gather 25-50 parts of fresh or dried crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers in a clean container.
Combine the gathered plant material with 50-60% ethanol in a ratio of 1:3 by weight for steeping.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a glass bottle and label it for storage in a cool dark place.

Barks: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and sedative properties, helping to reduce emotional pain and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh tilia platyphyllos leaves and flowers in the morning after sunrise to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content to 12%.
Combine the dried plant material with 500ml of vodka at a ratio of 1:5 to create the tincture base.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking to enhance extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

Additionally, rosemary's uplifting aroma can stimulate memory recall, enabling individuals to reflect on cherished moments with loved ones who have passed away. Adaptogenic properties: Rosemary's adaptogenic effects can help the body cope with stress and anxiety related to grief, allowing for a sense of emotional balance and resilience in the face of difficult emotions.

Flowers: Utilized for their emotional balancing and mood-enhancing properties to support individuals dealing with grief.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers at peak potency on a sunny morning.
Chop the rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers into small pieces using a sharp knife for best extraction.
Combine the chopped rosmarinus officinalis with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with grief because it has a profound impact on the emotional and spiritual well-being of an individual. As a result, sage tinctures can help alleviate feelings of sadness, anxiety, and despair that often accompany loss. Adaptogenic properties: Sage's adaptogenic compounds, including triterpenoids and phenolic acids, help the body adapt to stress and promote resilience, which can aid in coping with the emotional turmoil of grief.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Cleanly chop 250 grams of harvested salvia officinalis into smaller pieces to increase surface area.
Combine chopped salvia officinalis with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka and seal the mixture in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days in a cool dark place then strain the tincture through cheesecloth.
Bottle the tincture in amber glass bottles and label with ingredients dosages and preparation times.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi tinctures helps with grief because it has a profound impact on the mind and emotions. The herb is believed to enhance cognitive function, improve memory, and promote mental clarity, which can be especially beneficial during times of loss or bereavement when emotional overwhelm and disconnection from reality are common experiences. Brahmi's anxiolytic properties also help to reduce feelings of anxiety, stress, and agitation that often accompany grief, allowing the individual to process their emotions in a more calm and centered state.

Neuroprotective properties: Brahmi's neuroprotective compounds may help protect the brain from oxidative damage and inflammation caused by chronic stress and emotional trauma, which can contribute to grief and depression.

Cognitive enhancing properties: Brahmi tincture has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to improve cognitive function, memory, and concentration, which can help individuals process and come to terms with their emotions during the grieving process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, Brahmi roots help soothe emotional distress and calm the mind, making them an effective component for grief relief.
Seeds: The seeds contain a high amount of withanolides, which have been shown to reduce anxiety and depression-related symptoms, making them useful in managing feelings of sorrow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality bacopa monnieri leaves and stems from well-maintained cultivation practices ensuring potency.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material using air circulation and gentle stirring for three hours.
Combine one part dried bacopa monnieri with one part high-proof vodka in a glass jar, allowing it to steep for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, transferring the liquid tincture into a dark glass bottle after five minutes of pressing.
Store the finished tincture in a cool, dark place for six months before use, shaking the bottle daily during this time period.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with grief because its soothing and calming properties can ease the emotional turmoil that often accompanies loss. The menthol in peppermint oil can help to clarify and calm the mind, allowing individuals to process their emotions more effectively. Additionally, peppermint's refreshing and uplifting scent can help to boost mood and reduce feelings of sadness and despair, providing a sense of comfort and solace during a difficult time.
remedy_properties: Digestive aid: Peppermint's calming effects on the digestive system can soothe physical symptoms associated with grief, such as nausea, indigestion, and abdominal discomfort.

Anxiety and stress relief: Peppermint's natural anxiolytic properties can help alleviate feelings of anxiety, worry, and restlessness that often accompany grief, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.

Expectorant and anti-inflammatory: Peppermint's expectorant properties can help clear congestion and sinus pressure associated with grief-induced emotional turmoil, while its anti-inflammatory effects may reduce swelling and tension in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant are used in medicinal tinctures for grief due to their calming and soothing properties, which can help alleviate emotional distress.
Buds: Peppermint buds contain compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system, making them useful in tinctures for grief-related anxiety and stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves by cutting stems at dawn or early morning for optimal potency.
Chop the peppermint leaves into small pieces using sharp scissors or pruning shears to release oils.
Combine one part chopped peppermint leaves with five parts high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily to macerate the herbs.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture for future use.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Kansas coneflower tincture's antioxidant properties may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with emotional trauma, promoting a sense of calm and reducing feelings of despair.

Adaptogenic and anti-anxiety effects: The herb's adaptogenic properties may help the body cope with stress and anxiety related to grief, while its anti-anxiety effects can promote relaxation and reduce feelings of overwhelm.

Immune-supporting properties: Kansas coneflower tincture's immune-boosting properties may help alleviate symptoms of depression and anxiety that often accompany grief, by supporting overall physical and emotional well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their ability to calm and soothe emotions, helping to ease feelings of grief and sadness.
Fruits: The fruits contain a high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce stress and anxiety associated with grief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea angustifolia roots in the spring or fall, cutting them into small pieces.
Dry the cut root pieces in a warm dry place or using a food dehydrator on low setting for 8 hours.
Combine one part dried echinacea angustifolia root with three parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily for four to six weeks before straining and filtering.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles, storing them in the refrigerator at 2-3% dilution with water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with grief because they offer a gentle, yet profound, emotional support. The plant's calming properties help to soothe the nervous system, alleviating feelings of anxiety and overwhelm that often accompany loss. Additionally, elder's ability to balance emotions allows for a deeper processing of emotions, allowing individuals to work through their grief at a natural pace. As a result, herbal elder tinctures provide a sense of comfort and peace, helping to ease the painful journey of grieving and promoting emotional healing.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Elder tincture helps the body adapt to stress and emotional turmoil, reducing feelings of overwhelm and anxiety associated with grief. This allows for better emotional resilience and coping mechanisms.

Antidepressant and anti-anxiety compounds: Elder tincture contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to exhibit antidepressant and anxiolytic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of depression and anxiety often experienced during periods of grief.

Emollient and soothing properties: Elder tincture's emollient nature helps to calm and soothe the emotional body, reducing inflammation and irritability that can come with grieving. This can promote a sense of relaxation and tranquility.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, elder roots help calm emotional turmoil and soothe deep-seated sorrow.
Barks: With its unique combination of alkaloids and glycosides, elder bark is said to alleviate feelings of sadness and despair by promoting relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the sambucus nigra flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Combine 100g of dried sambucus nigra flowers with 500ml of high-proof vodka or brandy in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and maceration.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose tinctures helps with grief because it offers a sense of calm and tranquility, allowing individuals to process their emotions in a peaceful state. The herb's soothing properties can help ease feelings of sadness and anxiety, creating space for reflection and introspection. As the tincture promotes relaxation and reduces stress levels, it can also facilitate the release of pent-up emotions, making room for healing and acceptance to set in. This gentle yet powerful remedy can be a valuable companion on the journey of grief.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Beach rose tincture has adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to stress and emotional trauma, potentially reducing feelings of overwhelm and anxiety associated with grief.

Antidepressant and anti-anxiety effects: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in beach rose have been shown to possess antidepressant and anti-anxiety properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of depression and anxiety that often accompany grief.

Sedative and calming effects: Beach rose tincture contains sedative compounds like gallic acid, which can promote relaxation, reduce stress and anxiety, and help individuals cope with the emotional turmoil associated with grief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the beach rose plant have been traditionally used in herbal remedies to help alleviate feelings of sorrow and melancholy, making them an effective part for making tinctures for grief.
Barks: The barks of the beach rose plant contain compounds that have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to soothe emotional pain and promote healing from loss.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa x rugosa flowers and leaves from a pesticide-free garden or local market.
Dry the rosa x rugosa plant material using a food dehydrator or air drying method for 7-10 days.
Combine 500g of dried rosa x rugosa with 1000ml of organic vodka in a glass jar and steep for 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another glass container and discard solids after 24 hours.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store at room temperature for up to 2 years, with recommended dosage of 20-30 drops daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with grief because it has a profound ability to soothe and calm the emotional body. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties can ease feelings of overwhelm and anxiety, allowing individuals to process their emotions more clearly. Additionally, licorice root is known for its adaptogenic effects, helping the body cope with stress and promoting resilience. This natural support can empower individuals to work through their grief at a pace that feels authentic and healing. As a result, licorice tinctures can be a valuable ally on the journey of mourning.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Licorice root tincture helps to adapt the body to stress and promotes resilience, allowing individuals to better cope with emotional turmoil and feelings of grief.

Anxiolytic properties: The anxiolytic compounds in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin and isoliquiritigenin, can help alleviate anxiety and panic that often accompanies grief, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Licorice root's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the body and mind, which is common during times of grief, and promote overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are used in tincture-making because they contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps alleviate symptoms of emotional distress and promotes a sense of calm.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the licorice plant are used due to their ability to stimulate the adrenal glands, which can help regulate the body's response to stress and grief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried licorice root in the morning after dewfall when moisture content is lowest.
Chop 1 cup of harvested licorice root into small pieces for easier infusion.
Combine chopped licorice root with 4 cups of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a glass jar.
Steep mixture at room temperature for 6-8 weeks, shaking jar daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding solids and labeling final tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with grief because of its calming and soothing properties, which can provide emotional solace during difficult times. The tincture's flavonoids and antioxidants help to reduce inflammation and stress, promoting a sense of balance and equilibrium. Additionally, pot marigold's anti-anxiety effects can ease the overwhelming emotions that often accompany grief, allowing individuals to process their feelings more effectively and begin the healing process. By calming the mind and body, pot marigold tinctures can provide a sense of peace and hope during the grieving process.
remedy_properties: Antidepressant and mood-stabilizing effects: Calendula's flavonoids and terpenes have been shown to have a positive impact on neurotransmitter levels, helping to alleviate symptoms of depression and anxiety that often accompany grief.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Calendula's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to feelings of emotional pain and discomfort associated with grief.

Antioxidant and tissue-protective effects: Calendula's antioxidants can help protect tissues from oxidative damage caused by stress and trauma, supporting overall physical and emotional well-being during times of bereavement.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigolid plant have a calming effect, which helps to soothe and comfort individuals experiencing grief.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote emotional balance during times of grief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula flowers measuring one cup for every pint of vodka.
Combine the herb with vodka in a clean glass jar ensuring the herb is fully submerged.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place allowing it to steep for six weeks shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding solids and reserving the tincture.
Transfer the tincture to amber glass bottles labeling with date and dosage instructions and store in a dark place.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to grief that are treated with herbal tinctures are anxiety, depression, and stress. Tinctures of herbs like St. John's Wort, Ashwagandha, and Valerian root can help alleviate symptoms such as mood swings, irritability, and insomnia. These herbs have been traditionally used to support emotional well-being and promote a sense of calm and balance.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with grief is a blend of Ashwagandha, St. John's Wort, and Lavender. Ashwagandha supports emotional balance and stress relief, while St. John's Wort helps to alleviate symptoms of depression and anxiety associated with grief. Lavender promotes relaxation, calmness, and a sense of peace, making it easier to process and heal from emotional pain. This synergistic blend can help to soothe and calm the mind, body, and spirit, supporting the healing process and promoting emotional well-being.
